title: eremophila forrestii subsp. viridis
intro: Eremophila forrestii subsp. viridis, also known as the green emu bush, is a subspecies of the Eremophila forrestii plant, which is a flowering shrub native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Eremophila forrestii subsp. viridis include its potential to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Eremophila forrestii subsp. viridis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be dried and used in herbal remedies.
